pi_items.source: Live test: file exists at /home/ga/Documents/gretl_data/usa.gdt. GDT XML format with 103 quarterly observations (1984:1-2009:3), 2 variables (gdp, inf). Original source: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis FRED database (GDPC96, CPIAUCSL), bundled with Principles of Econometrics (Hill, Griffiths, Lim) Chapter 12 / POE5 textbook data.
Live test: confirmed file exists at this path. setup_task.sh copies from /opt/gretl_data/poe5/usa.gdt or /usr/share/gretl/data/poe5/usa.gdt, with embedded XML fallback.
Live test: confirmed directory /home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/ is writable by user ga. File creation tested successfully during live export_result.sh validation.
Live test: file creation and writing tested successfully. export_result.sh correctly checks this path.
Live test: directory exists, owned by ga:ga, starts empty after setup_task.sh cleanup. Permissions: drwxr-xr-x.

Live test via gretlcli: 'inf' is variable 2 in usa.gdt (CPI inflation rate, annualized %). 'gdp_growth' must be created by agent using 'series gdp_growth = 100 * diff(log(gdp))'. Both confirmed to work — gdp_growth generated as ID 3 with 102 valid observations (first obs is NA from differencing). gdp summary: mean=11536, range 7285-14774. inf summary: mean=2.81, range -6.3 to 5.6.

Live test: VAR estimated with 'var 2 gdp_growth inf' — gretl produces Equation 1: gdp_growth, Equation 2: inf. This ordering means variable 1 = gdp_growth, variable 2 = inf, which is consistent with the BQ restriction that shock 2 (demand) has zero long-run effect on variable 1 (output). Standard BQ convention confirmed via AER paper and textbook references.

Web search: Blanchard, O.J. and Quah, D. (1989), 'The Dynamic Effects of Aggregate Demand and Supply Disturbances,' American Economic Review, 79(4), 655-673. The BQ identification uses long-run restrictions on the structural impact matrix, specifically that the (1,2) element of the long-run structural matrix C(1)*S equals zero. Note: original paper used GNP growth + unemployment, not GDP growth + inflation, but the identification principle is identical and the GDP+inflation adaptation is widely used in textbooks.

pi_items.metadata_value: demand shock has zero cumulative effect on gdp_growth
pi_items.verified_value: demand shock has zero cumulative effect on gdp_growth
pi_items.source: Live test computation: C(1)*S matrix = [[1.6996, -3.3307e-16], [6.6721, 2.5631]]. Element [1,2] = -3.3307e-16 which is numerically zero (machine precision). This confirms the BQ long-run restriction holds: the second structural shock (demand) has zero cumulative long-run effect on the first variable (gdp_growth). Also confirmed via web search of the original BQ(1989) paper.

All keywords verified as relevant to the task. 'companion' refers to gretl's $compan matrix accessor (confirmed in live test: 'matrix Comp = $compan' works). 'cholesky' refers to the Cholesky decomposition of the long-run covariance matrix (confirmed: 'matrix P = cholesky(LR_cov)' works in gretl). 'structural', 'Blanchard-Quah', 'long-run', 'supply shock', 'demand shock' are all standard BQ SVAR terminology.

gdp_growth_creation.note: First observation (1984:1) is NA due to differencing

gdp_growth.interpretation: DOES NOT reject unit root at 5% (p=0.16). Borderline — GDP growth is theoretically stationary but the ADF test lacks power with this sample. Agent should note this but proceed.

inf.interpretation: Strongly rejects unit root at 1%. Inflation is stationary in this sample.

var_lag_selection.note: AIC selects lag 2, BIC and HQC select lag 1. Task says to use AIC, so correct answer is lag 2.

blanchard_quah_decomposition.verification: C(1)*S element [1,2] = -3.3307e-16 (numerically zero), confirming BQ restriction holds.
blanchard_quah_decomposition.method: S = inv(C1) * cholesky(C1 * Sigma * C1') where C1 = inv(I - A1 - A2)

gretl_specific.companion_matrix_structure: For VAR(p) with n=2 variables, companion is (2p x 2p). Lag coefficient matrices A_i are in rows 1:n, columns ((i-1)*n+1):(i*n).
gretl_specific.qform_function: qform(A, B) = A * B * A' — used for C1 * Sigma * C1'
gretl_specific.svar_addon_status: SVAR addon v2.0 bundled but INCOMPATIBLE with gretl 2022a (requires newer API). Task uses built-in matrix algebra instead.
gretl_specific.menu_path_var: Model > Multivariate time series > Vector Autoregression (VAR)
gretl_specific.menu_path_lag_selection: Model > Multivariate time series > VAR lag selection

privileged_info_summary: Dataset usa.gdt contains 103 quarterly observations (1984:1-2009:3) of real GDP (billions chained 2005 dollars, FRED: GDPC96) and CPI inflation rate (annualized %, FRED: CPIAUCSL). The agent must create gdp_growth = 100*diff(log(gdp)), yielding 102 valid observations starting 1984:2. ADF tests (4 lags, constant): gdp_growth tau_c = -2.342 (p=0.159, does NOT reject at 5%); inf tau_c = -3.931 (p=0.002, rejects). AIC-optimal VAR lag order is 2 (AIC=3.284); BIC and HQC both favor lag 1. VAR(2) sample is 1984:4-2009:3 (T=100). Key VAR(2) coefficients: gdp_growth equation has gdp_growth_1=0.457, gdp_growth_2=0.244; inf equation has inf_1=0.911, inf_2=-0.178. Granger causality: GDP growth Granger-causes inflation (F=3.15, p=0.047) but inflation does NOT Granger-cause GDP growth (F=1.03, p=0.360). For the BQ decomposition, the long-run multiplier matrix C(1) = inv(I - A1 - A2) = [[4.505, 0.572], [10.318, 5.051]]. The structural impact matrix S = [[0.283, -0.087], [0.743, 0.685]]. The long-run structural matrix C(1)*S = [[1.700, ~0], [6.672, 2.563]], confirming element [1,2] is zero (BQ restriction verified). The SVAR addon is bundled but incompatible with gretl 2022a; the agent must use built-in matrix algebra ($compan, $sigma, inv(), cholesky(), qform()). The VAR menu is at Model > Multivariate time series > Vector Autoregression.
